workLog.action: Read candidates JSON and selected issue #1728
workLog.evidence: feat/1728-autonomous branch pre-created by harness; P1 enhancement, no prior PR.

workLog.action: Explored repo structure and identified agent prompt templates and generator
workLog.evidence: Found templates/agents/*.shared.md (23 templates) generated via build/generate_agents.py to src/copilot-cli and src/vs-code-agents. Claude agents (src/claude/, .claude/agents/) are separately maintained and drift-checked by build/scripts/detect_agent_drift.py.
Durable state template covering Progress, Context Pressure levels, Decisions, Remaining Work, Handoff Notes, Signal to Orchestrator.

workLog.action: Added Context Budget Management section to all 23 shared agent templates
workLog.evidence: Includes budget awareness, degradation signals list, graceful degradation 4-step protocol, and pointer to issue #1726 for future hook enforcement.

workLog.action: Added section to all 23 Claude agent files in src/claude and .claude/agents
workLog.evidence: Hand-applied per detect_agent_drift.py guidance that Claude agents are not generated. 46 files updated.

workLog.action: Added Budget Estimation and Pre-Splitting addendum to orchestrator prompts
workLog.evidence: Table of task complexity -> budget -> model + worked example of pre-splitting a 200K-token architecture change into 5 sub-tasks.
generate_agents.py: 69 files generated, 0 errors. detect_agent_drift.py: 1 pre-existing drift (merge-resolver, not introduced by this change).

workLog.action: Replaced em-dash and en-dash characters in added sections per user style guide
workLog.evidence: Scope limited to the new Context Budget Management and Budget Estimation sections so pre-existing content is untouched.

nextSteps: Once issue #1726 hook infrastructure lands, wire PreCompact and PostToolUse hooks to enforce checkpointing automatically (reduces reliance on prompt-based compliance).
Consider a validator that greps agent prompts for Context Budget Management header to prevent regression.
Consider adding a markdown lint rule for em-dash across the whole repo to harmonize with user style guide.
